Unlike existing methods that rely on static optimization rules, Voyager dynamically generates and evaluates a wide range of algebraic transformation candidates, including novel reordering and fusion strategies tailored to graph characteristics. By leveraging symbolic graph attributes, Voyager efficiently estimates tensor shapes and prunes low-performance candidates. A runtime selection module further enhances performance by choosing the optimal transformation based on real-time input features. Our evaluation shows that Voyager achieves an average 3.5 × speedup on GPUs over the state-of-the-art works on representative GNNs and datasets.
items.abstract_zh:
图神经网络（GNN）在不同的应用领域中越来越受欢迎，并且变得越来越复杂。因此，实现高性能 GNN 执行至关重要。在各种技术中，包括算子重排序和算子融合在内的代数变换已成功应用于提高 DNN 模型的计算和内存访问效率。然而，直接将这些方法应用于 GNN 会导致性能不佳。这主要是由于输入图结构的多样性，使得预测性能优势变得具有挑战性。此外，这些输入图结构引入的复杂依赖关系限制了现有优化技术的有效性。

为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了 Voyager，这是一种专为 GNN 设计的输入自适应编译器。与依赖静态优化规则的现有方法不同，Voyager 动态生成并评估各种代数变换候选对象，包括针对图特征定制的新颖的重新排序和融合策略。通过利用符号图属性，Voyager 可以有效地估计张量形状并修剪低性能候选者。运行时选择模块通过根据实时输入特征选择最佳转换来进一步增强性能。我们的评估表明，与代表性 GNN 和数据集上的最先进作品相比，Voyager 在 GPU 上实现了平均 3.5 倍的加速。

items.title: TempGraph: An Efficient Chain-driven Temporal Graph Computing Framework on the GPU.
items.abstract: Tackling temporal path problems in temporal graphs is essential for time-sensitive applications. Although many solutions have been proposed to handle temporal path problems, due to the intrinsic time constraints, these solutions require the vertices of the temporal graph to be sequentially handled along the time-dependent chains (i.e., the temporal dependencies between these vertices) to form the temporal path. This sequential temporal nature poses the challenges of poor parallelism and slow convergence speed, preventing existing solutions from fully leveraging the massive parallelism and high internal bandwidth of GPU to handle temporal path problems. To overcome these challenges, this paper proposes TempGraph, an efficient chain-driven GPU-based temporal graph computing framework. Specifically, it transforms the temporal graph into a set of disjoint time-dependent chains that can elegantly expose the temporal dependency between the vertices while facilitating the fast path exploration along these chains over GPU. Furthermore, TempGraph employs a novel Generate-Activate-Compute execution model to decouple the temporal dependency between different chains through maintaining a set of shortcuts for them, which enables multiple chains to be concurrently handled by massive GPU threads, achieving fast convergence speed and high parallelism on the GPU. Experiments on an A100 GPU show that TempGraph outperforms the state-of-the-art GPU-based solutions by 3.0-16.2×. Besides, TempGraph on an A100 GPU gains 33.9-368.9× speedups compared to the cutting-edge CPU-based system TeGraph on a 128-core CPU machine.
items.abstract_zh: 解决时间图中的时间路径问题对于时间敏感的应用程序至关重要。尽管已经提出了许多解决方案来处理时间路径问题，但由于内在的时间限制，这些解决方案需要沿着时间相关链（即这些顶点之间的时间依赖性）顺序处理时间图的顶点以形成时间路径。这种顺序时间性质带来了并行性差和收敛速度慢的挑战，阻碍了现有解决方案充分利用 GPU 的大规模并行性和高内部带宽来处理时间路径问题。为了克服这些挑战，本文提出了 TempGraph，一种高效的链驱动的基于 GPU 的时态图计算框架。具体来说，它将时间图转换为一组不相交的时间相关链，可以优雅地揭示顶点之间的时间依赖性，同时促进通过 GPU 沿着这些链进行快速路径探索。此外，TempGraph采用了一种新颖的生成-激活-计算执行模型，通过维护一组快捷方式来解耦不同链之间的时间依赖性，使得多个链能够由大量GPU线程同时处理，实现快速收敛速度和GPU上的高并行性。 A100 GPU 上的实验表明，TempGraph 的性能比最先进的基于 GPU 的解决方案高出 3.0-16.2 倍。此外，与 128 核 CPU 机器上基于 CPU 的尖端系统 TeGraph 相比，A100 GPU 上的 TempGraph 获得了 33.9-368.9 倍的加速。

items.title: COGENT: Adaptable Compiler Toolchain for Tagging RISC-V Binaries.
items.abstract: Tags, or metadata, enrich software with domain-specific information that is consumed by hardware to enforce security and testing policies during runtime. However, given a target architecture, developing custom compilers that encode tags can be tedious and time-consuming. We present COGENT, a highly flexible and feature-rich compiler toolchain for instruction tag generation on the RISC-V architecture. Central to this effort is a LLVM-based compiler that is supplemented with a tag-aware disassembler and a tag integrity checker. COGENT is capable of: (a) generating tags at one or more of varying granularity (per function, per basic block, or per instruction), and (b) associating variable-width tags (1-32 bits) to instructions, and arbitrary-width tags to each function or basic block. Additionally, COGENT is capable of emitting control-flow labels, which are crucial in asserting control-flow integrity (CFI), a runtime property that aids in detecting bugs and exploits that violate control flow. We evaluate the correctness of tags generated by COGENT's compiler and the associated performance penalties, along with how well COGENT preserves IR-level tags at the lower level. We provide three exemplar applications-Control Flow Integrity, Adaptive Tracing, and Hardware-Level Function Tracing that can leverage COGENT. The tagged code incurs an average cycle count overhead from 5.24% to 0.94% in the worst and best cases, respectively, making it ideal for debugging and testing applications, including fuzzing.
items.abstract_zh: 标签或元数据通过硬件使用的特定于域的信息来丰富软件，以在运行时强制执行安全性和测试策略。然而，在给定目标架构的情况下，开发对标签进行编码的自定义编译器可能既乏味又耗时。我们推出 COGENT，这是一个高度灵活且功能丰富的编译器工具链，用于在 RISC-V 架构上生成指令标签。这项工作的核心是基于 LLVM 的编译器，并辅以标签感知反汇编器和标签完整性检查器。 COGENT 能够：(a) 以一种或多种不同粒度（每个函数、每个基本块或每个指令）生成标签，以及 (b) 将可变宽度标签（1-32 位）与指令相关联，并将任意宽度标签与每个函数或基本块相关联。此外，COGENT 能够发出控制流标签，这对于断言控制流完整性 (CFI) 至关重要，CFI 是一种运行时属性，有助于检测违反控制流的错误和漏洞利用。我们评估 COGENT 编译器生成的标签的正确性和相关的性能损失，以及 COGENT 在较低级别保留 IR 级别标签的情况。我们提供了三个可以利用 COGENT 的示例应用程序 - 控制流完整性、自适应跟踪和硬件级功能跟踪。在最坏和最好的情况下，标记代码的平均周期计数开销分别为 5.24% 到 0.94%，这使其成为调试和测试应用程序（包括模糊测试）的理想选择。

items.title: Hopps: Leveraging Sparsity to Accelerate Automata Processing.
items.abstract:
Automata processing (AP) is a key kernel in data analytics and scientific computing. AP workloads process a stream of symbols with many automata (FSMs) in parallel, e.g., pattern-matching network traffic against many malicious strings.

The need for high-performance AP has sparked the design of specialized accelerators. But prior AP accelerators are inefficient: AP workloads have substantial sparsity, but accelerators exploit no or limited sparsity. Specifically, each AP workload can be expressed as the concurrent traversal of all automata, which are encoded as graphs. But state-of-the-art accelerators store these graphs uncompressed, using bitsets. This allows the use of specialized memory crossbars that provide high parallelism and efficiency when graphs are dense. But many graphs are highly sparse, making crossbar-based accelerators inefficient.

We present Hopps, the first automata processing accelerator that exploits sparse data representations. Hopps combines two types of processing units: one represents data uncompressed, which achieves high throughput but is space-inefficient, while the other uses a compressed-sparse representation, which achieves high space efficiency but lower and more variable throughput. To use Hopps well, we present a novel automata mapping algorithm that maps most work to high-throughput units, while keeping a large fraction of state in space-efficient units. Hopps's hybrid design relaxes several constraints in crossbar-based designs, allowing for more efficient high-throughput units (e.g., by using a large number of smaller crossbars). Thus, by making the uncommon case cheap, Hopps makes the common case even faster.

We evaluate Hopps on AutomataZoo benchmarks. Hopps outperforms prior state-of-the-art accelerators Impala and SpAP by gmean 2.5x and 2.2x when using equal area.
items.abstract_zh:
自动机处理（AP）是数据分析和科学计算的关键核心。 AP 工作负载使用许多自动机 (FSM) 并行处理符号流，例如针对许多恶意字符串对网络流量进行模式匹配。

对高性能 AP 的需求激发了专用加速器的设计。但之前的 AP 加速器效率低下：AP 工作负载具有很大的稀疏性，但加速器没有利用稀疏性或利用有限的稀疏性。具体来说，每个AP工作负载可以表示为所有自动机的并发遍历，这些自动机被编码为图。但最先进的加速器使用位集来存储这些未压缩的图。这允许使用专门的内存交叉开关，在图形密集时提供高并行性和效率。但许多图高度稀疏，使得基于交叉开关的加速器效率低下。

我们推出了 Hopps，这是第一个利用稀疏数据表示的自动机处理加速器。 Hopps结合了两种类型的处理单元：一种表示未压缩的数据，可实现高吞吐量，但空间效率低下；另一种使用压缩稀疏表示，可实现高空间效率，但吞吐量较低且变化较大。为了很好地使用 Hopps，我们提出了一种新颖的自动机映射算法，该算法将大部分工作映射到高吞吐量单元，同时将大部分状态保留在节省空间的单元中。 Hopps 的混合设计放宽了基于交叉开关的设计中的几个限制，允许更高效的高吞吐量单元（例如，通过使用大量较小的交叉开关）。因此，通过使不常见的情况变得便宜，霍普斯使常见的情况变得更快。

我们在 AutomataZoo 基准测试上评估 Hopps。当使用相同面积时，Hopps 的性能比先前最先进的加速器 Impala 和 SpAP 的性能分别高出 gmean 2.5 倍和 2.2 倍。

items.title: Will We Ever have Truly Secure Operating Systems?
items.abstract:
Half a century after PSOS, the first attempts to prove an operating system (OS) secure, OS faults remain a major threat to computer systems security. A major step forward was the verification of the seL4 microkernel, the first proof of implementation correctness of an OS kernel. Over the next 4 years this proof was extended to the binary code, proofs of security enforcement, and sound and complete worst-case execution-time analysis. The proofs now cover 4 ISAs.

Yet, 15 years later, there is still no provably secure OS. While seL4 has been successfully deployed in defence and civilian security- and safety-critical systems, it is a microkernel that mostly guarantees process isolation without providing the application-oriented services expected from an OS. This not only makes seL4 difficult to deploy, but also means that there is limited assurance that a system built on top is secure in any real sense.

Why has seL4 not been leveraged into a secure OS? In this talk I explore some of the reasons behind this disappointing state of affairs, and what can be done about it. Specifically, I discuss our current work on LionsOS, a new seL4-based OS targeting the embedded/cyberphysical domain, which is easy to use and designed to be verifiable. We aim to achieve our goals by a principled design driven by simplicity, modularity, strict separation of concerns, and highly use-case specific policies, rather than the standard approach of trying to generalise policies.

I also discuss more speculative, early-stage work towards a provably secure, general-purpose OS
items.abstract_zh:
PSOS 首次尝试证明操作系统 (OS) 安全，半个世纪后，操作系统故障仍然是计算机系统安全的主要威胁。向前迈出的重要一步是对 seL4 微内核的验证，这是操作系统内核实现正确性的第一个证明。在接下来的 4 年里，这个证明扩展到了二进制代码、安全执行证明以及健全且完整的最坏情况执行时间分析。现在的证明涵盖了 4 个 ISA。

然而，15 年后，仍然没有可证明安全的操作系统。虽然 seL4 已成功部署在国防和民用安全关键系统中，但它是一个微内核，主要保证进程隔离，而不提供操作系统期望的面向应用程序的服务。这不仅使得 seL4 难以部署，而且还意味着构建在其之上的系统真正意义上的安全性的保证有限。

为什么 seL4 没有被利用到安全操作系统中？在这次演讲中，我探讨了这种令人失望的事态背后的一些原因，以及对此可以采取的措施。具体来说，我讨论了我们目前在 LionsOS 上的工作，这是一种基于 seL4 的新操作系统，针对嵌入式/网络物理领域，它易于使用且设计可验证。我们的目标是通过由简单性、模块化、严格的关注点分离和高度用例特定的策略驱动的原则性设计来实现我们的目标，而不是试图概括策略的标准方法。

我还讨论了更多推测性的早期工作，以实现可证明安全的通用操作系统

items.title: Wave: Offloading Resource Management to SmartNIC Cores.
items.abstract: SmartNICs are increasingly deployed in datacenters to offload tasks from server CPUs, improving the efficiency and flexibility of datacenter security, networking and storage. Optimizing cloud server efficiency in this way is critically important to ensure that virtually all server resources are available to paying customers. Userspace system software, specifically, decision-making tasks performed by various operating system subsystems, is particularly well suited for execution on mid-tier SmartNIC ARM cores. To this end, we introduce Wave, a framework for offloading userspace system software to processes/agents running on the SmartNIC. Wave uses Linux userspace systems to better align system functionality with SmartNIC capabilities. It also introduces a new host-SmartNIC communication API that enables offloading of even μs-scale system software. To evaluate Wave, we offloaded preexisting userspace system software including kernel thread scheduling, memory management, and an RPC stack to SmartNIC ARM cores, which showed a performance degradation of 1.1%-7.4% in an apples-to-apples comparison with on-host implementations. Wave recovered host resources consumed by on-host system software for memory management (saving 16 host cores), RPCs (saving 8 host cores), and virtual machines (an 11.2% performance improvement). Wave highlights the potential for rethinking system software placement in modern datacenters, unlocking new opportunities for efficiency and scalability.
items.abstract_zh: SmartNIC 越来越多地部署在数据中心中，以卸载服务器 CPU 的任务，从而提高数据中心安全、网络和存储的效率和灵活性。以这种方式优化云服务器效率对于确保几乎所有服务器资源可供付费客户使用至关重要。用户空间系统软件，特别是由各种操作系统子系统执行的决策任务，特别适合在中层 SmartNIC ARM 内核上执行。为此，我们引入了 Wave，一个用于将用户空间系统软件卸载到 SmartNIC 上运行的进程/代理的框架。 Wave 使用 Linux 用户空间系统来更好地将系统功能与 SmartNIC 功能结合起来。它还引入了新的主机-SmartNIC 通信 API，甚至可以卸载 μs 级的系统软件。为了评估 Wave，我们将现有的用户空间系统软件（包括内核线程调度、内存管理和 RPC 堆栈）卸载到 SmartNIC ARM 内核，与主机上实现进行比较时，性能下降了 1.1%-7.4%。 Wave 恢复了主机系统软件在内存管理（节省 16 个主机核心）、RPC（节省 8 个主机核心）和虚拟机（性能提升 11.2%）方面消耗的主机资源。 Wave 强调了重新思考现代数据中心中系统软件放置的潜力，释放了效率和可扩展性的新机会。

items.title: ASDR: Exploiting Adaptive Sampling and Data Reuse for CIM-based Instant Neural Rendering.
items.abstract:
Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF) offer significant promise for generating photorealistic images and videos. However, existing mainstream neural rendering models often fall short in meeting the demands for immediacy and power efficiency in practical applications. Specifically, these models frequently exhibit irregular access patterns and substantial computational overhead, leading to undesirable inference latency and high power consumption. Computing-in-memory (CIM), an emerging computational paradigm, has the potential to address these access bottlenecks and reduce the power consumption associated with model execution.

To bridge the gap between model performance and real-world scene requirements, we propose an algorithm-architecture co-design approach, abbreviated as ASDR, a CIM-based accelerator supporting efficient neural rendering. At the algorithmic level, we propose two rendering optimization schemes: (1) Dynamic sampling by online sensing of the rendering difficulty of different pixels, thus reducing access memory and computational overhead. (2) Reducing MLP overhead by decoupling and approximating the volume rendering of color and density. At the architecture level, we design an efficient ReRAM-based CIM architecture with efficient data mapping and reuse microarchitecture. Experiments demonstrate that our design can achieve up to 9.55× and 69.75× speedup over state-of-the-art NeRF accelerators and Xavier NX GPU in graphics rendering tasks with only 0.1 PSNR loss.
items.abstract_zh:
神经辐射场 (NeRF) 为生成逼真的图像和视频提供了重要的前景。然而，现有的主流神经渲染模型往往无法满足实际应用中即时性和功效的需求。具体来说，这些模型经常表现出不规则的访问模式和大量的计算开销，导致不期望的推理延迟和高功耗。内存计算 (CIM) 是一种新兴的计算范例，有可能解决这些访问瓶颈并降低与模型执行相关的功耗。

为了弥合模型性能和现实场景要求之间的差距，我们提出了一种算法架构协同设计方法，缩写为 ASDR，一种基于 CIM 的加速器，支持高效的神经渲染。在算法层面，我们提出了两种渲染优化方案：（1）通过在线感知不同像素的渲染难度进行动态采样，从而减少访问内存和计算开销。 (2)通过解耦和近似颜色和密度的体绘制来减少MLP开销。在架构层面，我们设计了一个高效的基于ReRAM的CIM架构，具有高效的数据映射和重用微架构。实验表明，在图形渲染任务中，我们的设计可以比最先进的 NeRF 加速器和 Xavier NX GPU 实现高达 9.55 倍和 69.75 倍的加速，而 PSNR 损失仅为 0.1。

items.title: λ-trim: Optimizing Function Initialization in Serverless Applications With Cost-driven Debloating.
items.abstract:
In this paper, we focus on an often-overlooked component of serverless application cold starts: monetary costs and Function Initialization. Traditionally considered the user's responsibility, Function Initialization is billable and accounts for more than 50% of the monetary cost associated with cold starts in real-world machine-learning applications.

We introduce λ-trim a system that optimizes Python serverless applications by eliminating redundant code while maintaining correctness. To maximize cost savings, λ-trim leverages the typical serverless pricing model to prioritize modules that significantly impact latency and memory usage. λ-trim features an automated pipeline comprising a static analyzer, a profiler specialized for the serverless pricing model, and a debloater. The optimized application can be directly deployed on serverless platforms, leading to substantial reductions in both latency and cost for cold starts.
items.abstract_zh:
在本文中，我们重点关注无服务器应用程序冷启动中经常被忽视的组成部分：货币成本和函数初始化。传统上，函数初始化被认为是用户的责任，它是可计费的，并且占实际机器学习应用程序中与冷启动相关的货币成本的 50% 以上。

我们引入了 λ-trim 系统，该系统通过消除冗余代码同时保持正确性来优化 Python 无服务器应用程序。为了最大限度地节省成本，λ-trim 利用典型的无服务器定价模型来优先考虑显着影响延迟和内存使用的模块。 λ-trim 具有一个自动化管道，包括静态分析器、专门用于无服务器定价模型的分析器和 debloater。优化后的应用程序可以直接部署在无服务器平台上，从而大幅降低冷启动的延迟和成本。

items.title: The ASPLOS 2025 / EuroSys 2025 Contest on Intra-Operator Parallelism for Distributed Deep Learning.
items.abstract: A chief enabler of large-scale deep learning is the distribution of computation across multiple interconnected hardware accelerators. In order to unlock the maximum possible performance, a compiler must first select a reasonable strategy to parallelize a model's operations. Since neural network architectures admit multiple flavors of parallelism, determining the proper strategy for each instruction is a critical (albeit non-trivial) task. To solicit new ideas toward solving this challenging combinatorial optimization problem, we organized the ASPLOS 2025 / EuroSys 2025 Contest on Intra-Operator Parallelism for Distributed Deep Learning, a multi-month competition focused on advancing the state-of-the-art for model partitioning algorithms. In this paper, we offer a retrospective of this event, including the basic problem formulation, key challenges & opportunities, our new benchmark suite, and the quality of submissions received.
items.abstract_zh: 大规模深度学习的主要推动者是跨多个互连硬件加速器的计算分布。为了释放最大可能的性能，编译器必须首先选择合理的策略来并行化模型的操作。由于神经网络架构允许多种并行方式，因此为每条指令确定正确的策略是一项关键（尽管很重要）的任务。为了征求解决这一具有挑战性的组合优化问题的新想法，我们组织了 ASPLOS 2025 / EuroSys 2025 分布式深度学习算子内并行性竞赛，这是一项为期数月的竞赛，专注于推进模型划分算法的最新技术。在本文中，我们回顾了这一活动，包括基本问题的表述、主要挑战和机遇、我们的新基准套件以及收到的提交的质量。

items.title: PowerMove: Optimizing Compilation for Neutral Atom Quantum Computers with Zoned Architecture.
items.abstract: Neutral atom quantum computers (NAQCs) have emerged as promising candidates for scalable quantum computing, thanks to their advanced hardware capabilities, particularly qubit movement and the Zoned Architecture (ZA). However, fully harnessing these features presents significant compilation challenges, requiring careful coordination across gate scheduling, qubit positioning, atom movement, and inter-zone communication. In this paper, we propose PowerMove, an efficient compiler for NAQCs that unlocks new optimization opportunities, significantly improving qubit movement strategies while seamlessly integrating ZA. Our evaluation demonstrates orders-of-magnitude fidelity improvements over state-of-the-art methods, with execution time reduced by up to 3.76× and compilation time accelerated by up to 216.9× across various NISQ applications. Furthermore, PowerMove extends naturally to the fault-tolerant quantum computing (FTQC) setting, where physical qubits are replaced by logical qubits encoded in QEC codes, achieving a 4.78× reduction in execution time. These results highlight PowerMove's impact on both near-term NISQ applications and long-term FTQC implementations. We have open-sourced our codes at https://github.com/Scarlett0815/PowerMove to facilitate further research and collaboration within the community.
items.abstract_zh: 中性原子量子计算机（NAQC）凭借其先进的硬件功能，特别是量子位移动和分区架构（ZA），已成为可扩展量子计算的有希望的候选者。然而，充分利用这些功能带来了巨大的编译挑战，需要在门调度、量子位定位、原子移动和区域间通信之间进行仔细协调。在本文中，我们提出了 PowerMove，这是一种用于 NAQC 的高效编译器，可以释放新的优化机会，显着改进量子位移动策略，同时无缝集成 ZA。我们的评估表明，与最先进的方法相比，保真度得到了数量级的改进，各种 NISQ 应用程序的执行时间缩短了 3.76 倍，编译时间加快了 216.9 倍。此外，PowerMove 自然地扩展到容错量子计算 (FTQC) 设置，其中物理量子位被 QEC 代码中编码的逻辑量子位取代，从而实现执行时间减少 4.78 倍。这些结果凸显了 PowerMove 对近期 NISQ 应用和长期 FTQC 实施的影响。我们在 https://github.com/Scarlett0815/PowerMove 开源了我们的代码，以促进社区内的进一步研究和协作。

items.title: SYLQ-SV: Scaling Symbolic Execution of Hardware Designs with Query Caching.
items.abstract: Symbolic execution of hardware designs is a path-based analysis that can deliver high quality coverage and security verification results. Unfortunately, the technique has historically struggled with the path explosion problem and, despite recent advances, remains expensive. We present SylQ-SV , a dedicated SystemVerilog symbolic execution engine that uses SMT query caching to improve execution times, reducing run time by up to 17% over the state of the art. SylQ-SV provides language support for all necessary SystemVerilog constructs, including SystemVerilog Assertions, to provide end-to-end verification workflows of the open-source designs most commonly appearing in the literature. We evaluate SYLQ-SV on the OR1200 CPU, the OpenTitan SoC with Ibex core, and two SoC designs from the HACK@DAC competitions (with PULPissimo core and CVA6 core, respectively). We make the SylQ-SV source code and all data used in the evaluation publicly available.
items.abstract_zh: 硬件设计的符号执行是基于路径的分析，可以提供高质量的覆盖和安全验证结果。不幸的是，该技术历来一直与路径爆炸问题作斗争，并且尽管最近取得了进展，但仍然昂贵。我们推出了 SylQ-SV，这是一种专用的 SystemVerilog 符号执行引擎，它使用 SMT 查询缓存来缩短执行时间，与现有技术相比，运行时间最多可缩短 17%。 SylQ-SV provides language support for all necessary SystemVerilog constructs, including SystemVerilog Assertions, to provide end-to-end verification workflows of the open-source designs most commonly appearing in the literature. We evaluate SYLQ-SV on the OR1200 CPU, the OpenTitan SoC with Ibex core, and two SoC designs from the HACK@DAC competitions (with PULPissimo core and CVA6 core, respectively).我们公开 SylQ-SV 源代码和评估中使用的所有数据。

items.title: HybridTier: an Adaptive and Lightweight CXL-Memory Tiering System.
items.abstract: Modern workloads are demanding increasingly larger memory capacity. Compute Express Link (CXL)-based memory tiering has emerged as a promising solution for addressing this problem by utilizing traditional DRAM alongside slow-tier CXL memory devices. We analyze prior tiering systems and observe two challenges for high-performance memory tiering: adapting to skewed but dynamically varying data hotness distributions while minimizing memory and cache overhead due to tiering. To address these challenges, we propose HybridTier, an adaptive and lightweight tiering system for CXL memory. HybridTier tracks both long-term data access frequency and short-term access momentum simultaneously to accurately capture and adapt to shifting hotness distributions. HybridTier reduces the metadata memory overhead by tracking data accesses probabilistically, obtaining higher memory efficiency by trading off a small amount of tracking inaccuracy that has a negligible impact on application performance. To reduce cache overhead, HybridTier uses lightweight data structures that optimize for data locality to track data hotness. Our evaluations show that HybridTier outperforms prior systems by up to 91% (19% geomean), incurring 2.0-7.8x less memory overhead and 1.7-3.5x less cache misses.
items.abstract_zh: 现代工作负载需要越来越大的内存容量。基于 Compute Express Link (CXL) 的内存分层已成为解决此问题的一种有前途的解决方案，它利用传统 DRAM 和慢速层 CXL 内存设备。我们分析了现有的分层系统，并观察了高性能内存分层的两个挑战：适应倾斜但动态变化的数据热度分布，同时最大限度地减少分层带来的内存和缓存开销。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了 HybridTier，这是一种适用于 CXL 内存的自适应轻量级分层系统。 HybridTier 同时跟踪长期数据访问频率和短期访问动量，以准确捕获和适应不断变化的热度分布。 HybridTier 通过概率跟踪数据访问来减少元数据内存开销，通过权衡对应用程序性能影响可以忽略不计的少量跟踪不准确性来获得更高的内存效率。为了减少缓存开销，HybridTier 使用针对数据局部性进行优化的轻量级数据结构来跟踪数据热度。我们的评估表明，HybridTier 的性能比之前的系统高出 91%（19% geomean），内存开销减少了 2.0-7.8 倍，缓存未命中减少了 1.7-3.5 倍。

items.title: Neuralink: Fast on-Device LLM Inference with Neuron Co-Activation Linking.
items.abstract:
Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable success across various domains, yet deploying them on mobile devices remains an arduous challenge due to their extensive computational and memory demands. While lightweight LLMs have been developed to fit mobile environments, they suffer from degraded model accuracy. In contrast, sparsity-based techniques minimize DRAM usage by selectively transferring only relevant neurons to DRAM while retaining the full model in external storage, such as flash. However, such approaches are critically limited by numerous I/O operations, particularly on smartphones with severe IOPS constraints.

In this paper, we propose Neuralink , a novel approach that accelerates LLM inference on smartphones by optimizing neuron placement in flash memory. Neuralink leverages the concept of Neuron Co-Activation, where neurons frequently activated together are linked to facilitate continuous read access and optimize I/O efficiency. Our approach incorporates a two-stage solution: an offline stage that reorganizes neuron placement based on co-activation patterns, and an online stage that employs tailored data access and caching strategies to align well with hardware characteristics. Evaluations conducted on a variety of smartphones and LLMs demonstrate that Neuralink achieves on average 1.49x improvements in end-to-end latency compared to the state-of-the-art. As the first solution to optimize storage placement under sparsity, Neuralink explores a new optimization space at the intersection of sparsity-driven algorithm and storage-level system co-design for LLM inference.
items.abstract_zh:
大型语言模型 (LLM) 在各个领域取得了显着的成功，但由于其广泛的计算和内存需求，将它们部署在移动设备上仍然是一项艰巨的挑战。虽然轻量级法学硕士已经被开发来适应移动环境，但它们的模型准确性下降。相比之下，基于稀疏性的技术通过有选择地仅将相关神经元传输到 DRAM，同时将完整模型保留在外部存储（例如闪存）中，最大限度地减少 DRAM 使用。然而，此类方法受到大量 I/O 操作的严重限制，特别是在具有严格 IOPS 限制的智能手机上。

在本文中，我们提出了 Neuralink，这是一种通过优化闪存中的神经元放置来加速智能手机上的 LLM 推理的新颖方法。 Neuralink 利用神经元共激活的概念，将频繁激活的神经元连接在一起，以促进连续读取访问并优化 I/O 效率。我们的方法采用了两阶段解决方案：离线阶段根据共激活模式重新组织神经元布局，在线阶段采用定制的数据访问和缓存策略以与硬件特性很好地保持一致。对各种智能手机和法学硕士进行的评估表明，与最先进的技术相比，Neuralink 的端到端延迟平均提高了 1.49 倍。作为第一个在稀疏性下优化存储布局的解决方案，Neuralink 在稀疏性驱动算法和 LLM 推理的存储级系统协同设计的交叉点上探索了一个新的优化空间。

items.title: DejaVuzz: Disclosing Transient Execution Bugs with Dynamic Swappable Memory and Differential Information Flow Tracking Assisted Processor Fuzzing.
items.abstract:
Transient execution vulnerabilities have emerged as a critical threat to modern processors. Hardware fuzzing testing techniques have recently shown promising results in discovering transient execution bugs in large-scale out-of-order processor designs. However, their poor microarchitectural controllability and observability prevent them from effectively and efficiently detecting transient execution vulnerabilities.

This paper proposes DejaVuzz, a novel pre-silicon stage processor transient execution bug fuzzer. DejaVuzz utilizes two innovative operating primitives: dynamic swappable memory and differential information flow tracking, enabling more effective and efficient transient execution vulnerability detection. The dynamic swappable memory enables the isolation of different instruction streams within the same address space. Leveraging this capability, DejaVuzz generates targeted training for arbitrary transient windows and eliminates ineffective training, enabling efficient triggering of diverse transient windows. The differential information flow tracking aids in observing the propagation of sensitive data across the microarchitecture. Based on taints, DejaVuzz designs the taint coverage matrix to guide mutation and uses taint liveness annotations to identify exploitable leakages. Our evaluation shows that DejaVuzz outperforms the state-of-the-art fuzzer SpecDoctor, triggering more comprehensive transient windows with lower training overhead and achieving a 4.7x coverage improvement. And DejaVuzz also mitigates control flow over-tainting with acceptable overhead and identifies 5 previously undiscovered transient execution vulnerabilities (with 6 CVEs assigned) on BOOM and XiangShan.
items.abstract_zh:
瞬态执行漏洞已成为现代处理器的严重威胁。硬件模糊测试技术最近在发现大规模无序处理器设计中的瞬时执行错误方面显示出了有希望的结果。然而，它们较差的微架构可控性和可观察性使得它们无法有效且高效地检测瞬时执行漏洞。

本文提出了 DejaVuzz，一种新型的硅前级处理器瞬态执行错误模糊器。 DejaVuzz 利用两种创新的操作原语：动态可交换内存和差异信息流跟踪，从而实现更有效和高效的瞬时执行漏洞检测。动态可交换内存可以隔离同一地址空间内的不同指令流。利用这一功能，DejaVuzz 可以为任意瞬态窗口生成有针对性的训练，并消除无效的训练，从而实现多种瞬态窗口的高效触发。差异信息流跟踪有助于观察敏感数据在微架构中的传播。基于污点，DejaVuzz 设计了污点覆盖矩阵来指导突变，并使用污点活性注释来识别可利用的泄漏。我们的评估表明，DejaVuzz 的性能优于最先进的模糊器 SpecDoctor，能够以更低的训练开销触发更全面的瞬态窗口，并实现 4.7 倍的覆盖率提升。 DejaVuzz 还以可接受的开销减轻了控制流过度污染，并在 BOOM 和香山上识别了 5 个以前未发现的瞬时执行漏洞（分配了 6 个 CVE）。

items.title: Fault Escaping: Improving Robustness of DPU Enhanced Platform with Mutual Assisted VM Recovery.
items.abstract:
Modern cloud servers achieve significant performance improvements by exploiting data processing units (DPUs) to offload virtualization overhead. Unlike traditional monolithic hypervisors, this offloading approach splits VM state and distributes hypervisor functions across the DPU and the Host, transforming the cloud server from a single system into a sophisticated orchestration of multiple self-managed processing units. However, the failure rate of these systems has significantly increased, as errors from either the DPU or the Host can crash the entire machine. All these changes necessitate a comprehensive revamp of current VM fault tolerance and isolation mechanisms.

This paper explores a novel approach to VM fault tolerance by treating the split hypervisors on the DPU SoC and the Host as redundant peers. We propose a fault-escaping scheme that enables VMs to escape from a failing SoC or Host. With hybrid synchronization, the entire VM state becomes fully accessible on either the SoC or the Host with minimum synchronization overhead (in kilobytes). Instead of recovering the failed hypervisor, this scheme migrates the verified VM state, allowing the VM to escape from the failure and resume on a new machine. The evaluation demonstrates that the escape-based mechanism introduces no runtime performance overhead, achieves performance levels comparable to VM live migration, and reduces the VM recovery time objective (RTO) by 4x to 14x in one of the largest cloud production environments.
items.abstract_zh:
现代云服务器通过利用数据处理单元 (DPU) 卸载虚拟化开销，实现了显着的性能改进。与传统的整体虚拟机管理程序不同，这种卸载方法可分割虚拟机状态并在 DPU 和主机之间分配虚拟机管理程序功能，从而将云服务器从单一系统转变为多个自管理处理单元的复杂编排。然而，这些系统的故障率显着增加，因为 DPU 或主机的错误可能导致整个机器崩溃。所有这些变化都需要对当前虚拟机容错和隔离机制进行全面改造。

本文通过将 DPU SoC 和主机上的拆分虚拟机管理程序视为冗余对等点，探索了一种新的 VM 容错方法。我们提出了一种故障逃逸方案，使虚拟机能够逃离发生故障的 SoC 或主机。通过混合同步，整个 VM 状态可以在 SoC 或主机上完全访问，同步开销最小（以千字节为单位）。该方案不是恢复发生故障的虚拟机管理程序，而是迁移经过验证的虚拟机状态，从而允许虚拟机摆脱故障并在新机器上恢复。评估表明，基于转义的机制不会引入运行时性能开销，实现了与虚拟机实时迁移相当的性能水平，并将虚拟机恢复时间目标 (RTO) 在最大的云生产环境之一中降低了 4 倍到 14 倍。

items.title: PUSHtap: PIM-based In-Memory HTAP with Unified Data Storage Format.
items.abstract: Hybrid transaction/analytical processing (HTAP) is an emerging database paradigm that supports both online transaction processing (OLTP) and online analytical processing (OLAP) workloads. Computing-intensive OLTP operations, involving row-wise data manipulation, are suitable for row-store format. In contrast, memory-intensive OLAP operations, which are column-centric, benefit from column-store format. This data-format dilemma prevents HTAP systems from concurrently achieving three design goals: performance isolation, data freshness, and workload-specific optimization. Another background technology is Processing-in-Memory (PIM), which integrates computing units (PIM units) inside DRAM memory devices to accelerate memory-intensive workloads, including OLAP. Our key insight is to combine the interleaved CPU access and localized PIM unit access to provide two-dimensional access to address the data format contradictions inherent in HTAP. First, we propose a unified data storage format with novel data alignment and placement techniques to optimize the effective bandwidth of CPUs and PIM units and exploit the PIM's parallelism. Second, we implement the multi-version concurrency control (MVCC) essential for single-instance HTAP. Third, we extend the commercial PIM architecture to support the OLAP operations and concurrent access from PIM and CPU. Experiments show that PUSHtap can achieve 3.4X/4.4X OLAP/OLTP throughput improvement compared to multi-instance PIM-based design.
items.abstract_zh: 混合事务/分析处理 (HTAP) 是一种新兴的数据库范例，支持联机事务处理 (OLTP) 和联机分析处理 (OLAP) 工作负载。计算密集型 OLTP 操作涉及行数据操作，适合行存储格式。相反，以列为中心的内存密集型 OLAP 操作受益于列存储格式。这种数据格式的困境阻碍了 HTAP 系统同时实现三个设计目标：性能隔离、数据新鲜度和特定于工作负载的优化。另一项背景技术是内存处理（PIM），它将计算单元（PIM单元）集成在DRAM内存设备内部，以加速内存密集型工作负载，包括OLAP。我们的主要见解是将交错的 CPU 访问和本地化 PIM 单元访问结合起来，提供二维访问，以解决 HTAP 固有的数据格式矛盾。首先，我们提出了一种统一的数据存储格式，具有新颖的数据对齐和放置技术，以优化 CPU 和 PIM 单元的有效带宽并利用 PIM 的并行性。其次，我们实现了单实例 HTAP 所必需的多版本并发控制（MVCC）。第三，我们扩展了商业PIM架构以支持OLAP操作以及PIM和CPU的并发访问。实验表明，与基于多实例 PIM 的设计相比，PUSHtap 可以实现 3.4 倍/4.4 倍的 OLAP/OLTP 吞吐量提升。

items.title: DeepContext: A Context-aware, Cross-platform, and Cross-framework Tool for Performance Profiling and Analysis of Deep Learning Workloads.
items.abstract: Effective performance optimization of deep learning models requires comprehensive profiling across heterogeneous computing environments, yet existing tools fail to bridge the semantic gap between high-level operations and low-level execution. This paper presents DeepContext, a novel profiling system that correlates program contexts across Python code, deep learning frameworks, C/C++ libraries, and GPU execution. DeepContext features a framework-agnostic shim layer that seamlessly correlates the behavior of the deep learning framework with hardware performance metrics. Furthermore, DeepContext provides an automated performance analyzer that offers actionable optimization guidance based on its holistic view of the entire software stack of deep learning applications. DeepContext works for mainstream deep learning frameworks and runs on modern CPU+GPU architectures with low overhead. Our evaluation demonstrates that DeepContext uncovers previously hidden performance bottlenecks in real-world deep-learning applications. Guided by DeepContext, we are able to fix multiple performance issues, achieving speed-ups between 1.06× and 1.66×.
items.abstract_zh: 深度学习模型的有效性能优化需要跨异构计算环境进行全面的分析，但现有工具无法弥合高级操作和低级执行之间的语义差距。本文介绍了 DeepContext，这是一种新颖的分析系统，可将 Python 代码、深度学习框架、C/C++ 库和 GPU 执行之间的程序上下文关联起来。 DeepContext 具有与框架无关的填充层，可将深度学习框架的行为与硬件性能指标无缝关联。此外，DeepContext 还提供了一个自动化性能分析器，该分析器根据深度学习应用程序整个软件堆栈的整体视图提供可行的优化指导。 DeepContext 适用于主流深度学习框架，运行在现代 CPU+GPU 架构上，开销较低。我们的评估表明 DeepContext 揭示了现实世界深度学习应用程序中以前隐藏的性能瓶颈。在 DeepContext 的指导下，我们能够修复多个性能问题，实现 1.06 倍到 1.66 倍的加速。

items.title: Syno: Structured Synthesis for Neural Operators.
items.abstract: The desires for better prediction accuracy and higher execution performance in neural networks never end. Neural architecture search (NAS) and tensor compilers are two popular techniques to optimize these two goals, but they are both limited to composing or optimizing existing manually designed operators rather than coming up with completely new designs. In this work, we explore the less studied direction of neural operator synthesis, which aims to automatically and efficiently discover novel neural operators with better accuracy and/or speed. We develop an end-to-end framework Syno, to realize practical neural operator synthesis. Syno makes use of a novel set of fine-grained primitives defined on tensor dimensions, which ensure various desired properties to ease model training, and also enable expression canonicalization techniques to avoid redundant candidates during search. Syno further adopts a novel guided synthesis flow to obtain valid operators matched with the specified input/output dimension sizes, and leverages efficient stochastic tree search algorithms to quickly explore the design space. We demonstrate that Syno discovers better operators with average speedups of 1.37× to 2.06× on various hardware and compiler choices, while keeping less than 1% accuracy loss even on NAS-optimized models.
items.abstract_zh: 对神经网络更好的预测精度和更高的执行性能的渴望永无止境。神经架构搜索（NAS）和张量编译器是优化这两个目标的两种流行技术，但它们都仅限于组合或优化现有的手动设计的运算符，而不是提出全新的设计。在这项工作中，我们探索了较少研究的神经算子合成方向，其目的是自动有效地发现具有更高准确性和/或速度的新型神经算子。我们开发了一个端到端框架Syno，来实现实用的神经算子合成。 Syno 利用在张量维度上定义的一组新颖的细粒度原语，这确保了各种所需的属性以简化模型训练，并且还启用表达式规范化技术以避免搜索过程中出现冗余候选者。 Syno进一步采用新颖的引导综合流程来获取与指定输入/输出维度大小匹配的有效算子，并利用高效的随机树搜索算法来快速探索设计空间。我们证明了 Syno 发现了更好的算子，在各种硬件和编译器选择上平均加速为 1.37× 到 2.06×，同时即使在 NAS 优化模型上也能保持低于 1% 的精度损失。
